title: A Quiet Place: Day One IMAX Box Office Performance
text: The prequel film opened strongly on IMAX screens globally, generating roughly $12.5 million in IMAX revenue during its debut weekend. This impressive performance contributed to the movie's overall worldwide opening of nearly $100 million across all formats. In North America, the film earned $5.8 million from 408 IMAX locations, representing 11% of the total IMAX box office. International markets contributed an additional $3.5 million, with China accounting for $1 million of that amount. Directed by Michael Sarnoski rather than franchise creator John Krasinski, the movie serves as a prequel exploring the arrival of sound-sensitive extraterrestrials in New York City. The cast includes Lupita Nyong'o and Joseph Quinn in leading roles. The film still has significant growth potential, as it has yet to release in several key Latin American markets including Argentina, Chile, and Ecuador. Early word-of-mouth reception has been positive, suggesting strong staying power in theaters.
